body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
As opposed to in the bucket model's step (which is how it is done now). This version continues to use the `CL.turbulent_fluxes!` method, as opposed to #1816 which copies the internals of that function into the coupler and as a result is much more complicated.

Also adds some functions to simplify the flux calculation code structure.

I find it odd that we were calling `CL.turbulent_fluxes!` from the bucket's `compute_surface_fluxes!` method, even though the fluxes were already computed in the bucket's step. I expect this was just extra computation but didn't change the values.

Requires https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/pull/1677 + land release

## Content
- [x] Add `get_roughness_params` to reduce code duplication
- [x] Add `update_flux_fields!` to split work into smaller functions with clear roles (and reduce code duplication)
- [x] Use new land branch, where fluxes aren't computed in `step!`

## Dependency updates
```
    Updating `~/clima/ClimaCoupler.jl/experiments/CMIP/Project.toml`
  [b2c96348] ↑ ClimaAtmos v0.38.0 ⇒ v0.38.1
  [1ecacbb8] ↑ ClimaDiagnostics v0.3.3 ⇒ v0.3.4
  [08f4d4ce] ↑ ClimaLand v1.6.5 ⇒ v1.7.0
  [0376089a] ↑ ClimaOcean v0.9.4 ⇒ v0.9.5
  [85f8d34a] ↑ NCDatasets v0.14.14 ⇒ v0.14.15
```
I accidentally switched the `Manifest.toml` files from julia 1.10 to 1.12 earlier this week, so this PR switches them back to 1.10.

- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included

- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

title: compute bucket fluxes in compute_surface_fluxes
